articles.title: Microsoft wants lawyers to trust its new AI agent in Word documents
articles.description:
Microsoft is launching a new AI agent inside Word that's specifically designed for legal teams. Legal Agent handles document edits, negotiation history, and complex documents to help legal teams handle tasks like reviewing contracts.
"Instead of relying on general AI models to interpret commands, the agent follows structured workflows shaped by real legal practice, managing clearly defined, repeatable tasks like reviewing contracts clause by clause against a playbook," explains Sumit Chauhan, corporate vice president of Microsoft's Office Product Group. 
The Legal Agent can work with existing documents that have tracked changes, and analyz …

Ugandan opposition figures, human rights organisations and legal experts have condemned a sweeping bill that proposes up to 20 years in prison for promoting “foreign interests”, and imposes restrictions on a broad range of people and organisations that work with or receive funding from overseas partners.
The protection of sovereignty bill 2026 is being fast tracked through parliament, with debate expected to conclude before the presidential swearing-in on 12 May.

articles.title: Dyson finally made a better robot, but a worse vacuum
articles.description:
The Dyson Spot + Scrub Ai is Dyson’s first combination robot vacuum and mop. | Photo by Jennifer Pattison Tuohy / The Verge	

I'm deeply conflicted about the Dyson Spot + Scrub Ai robot vacuum and mop. It's the company's best robotic floor cleaner to date, with excellent mopping performance, good navigation and obstacle detection, and a multifunction dock that takes much of the busywork off your hands. But Dyson's first attempt at a vacuum-and-mop combo is a worse vacuum than its predecessors, and that's because there's no Dyson motor in this vacuum.
You read that right. For $1,200, the Spot + Scrub doesn't get you a Dyson vacuum; instead, you're getting a third-party motor and brush system. "It's not one of our V10 motors, it's one of our partner technologies," N …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Dyson put someone else’s motor in its robot vacuum
articles.description:
Dyson’s newest robot vacuum adds mopping capabilities but takes away the Dyson motor of its predecessor. | Photo by Jennifer Pattison Tuohy / The Verge	

Dyson, a company built on engineering high-speed motors, has confirmed to The Verge that its newest robot vacuum doesn't use a Dyson motor. The recently launched Spot & Scrub Ai robot vacuum and mop was "co-engineered," Nathan Lawson McLean, senior design manager at Dyson, told The Verge. 
According to Lawson McLean, the device merges "new and already existing Dyson technologies with other platforms." Specifically, the new lidar-based navigation tech and the robot's vacuum motor were developed by a third party. "It's not one of our V10 motors; it's one of our partner technologies," said Lawson McLean.

"It's not one of our V10 motors, it's  …

Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Trump’s mass firing just dealt another blow to American science
articles.description: This past week delivered another gut punch for science in the US. This time, the target was the National Science Foundation—a federal agency that funds major research projects to the tune of around $9 billion. The foundation’s efforts were overseen by a board of 22 prominent scientists. On Friday last week, they were all fired.…

articles.title: A new T-Mobile network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
articles.description: A new US-wide cell phone network marketed to Christians is set to launch next week. It blocks porn, which experts in network security say marks the first time a US cell plan has used network-level blocking for such content that can’t be turned off even by adult account owners. It’s also rolling out a filter…

articles.title: Advocates demand High Court circuit bench for central region of State in Shivamogga
articles.description: Advocates in Shivamogga oppose setting up a bench in Mangaluru covering the coastal and Malnad districts, including Shivamogga

articles.title: Explained: Tamil Nadu elections and the long gap between polling and counting
articles.description: Tamil Nadu’s Assembly elections have often seen long gaps between polling and the counting of votes. This video looks at how election schedules in the state have evolved since 1967, the reasons behind delays, and the political moments that shaped modern Tamil Nadu.

articles.title: Smoothie King plots expansion as wellness trends boost sales
articles.description: Smoothie King's system-wide sales have climbed 64% over the last five years.

articles.title: 5-year-old aspiring astronaut Jack says "it was fun" to watch historic Artemis launch
articles.description: Jack, a 5-year-old aspiring astronaut, talks to "CBS Mornings" about watching the Artemis II launch in person in Florida.

articles.title: Street artist Banksy confirms mystery statue in Central London is his work, says ‘there was a bit of a gap’
articles.description: Banksy confirms that a large statue in Waterloo Place is his work, drawing crowds and praise. Installed overnight, it depicts a suited man with a flag over his face. Authorities are protecting it, while the piece adds to his series of bold, political artworks across London.

articles.title: Noida International Airport to begin commercial flights from 15 June; IndiGo to operate first flight
articles.description: Noida International Airport's commercial flights will commence on 15 June, with IndiGo as the inaugural operator. The facility aims to enhance regional connectivity and tourism, featuring modern infrastructure.

articles.title: TDP chief Palla Srinivasa Rao welcomes State government’s move to feature Class X toppers from governement schools
articles.description: The Gajuwaka MLA said the recognition would shape student confidence and serve as a source of social inspiration, particularly for those from disadvantaged backgrounds

articles.title: WATCH:  NYPD praises officers after home explosion caught on camera
articles.description: Seven NYPD officers are recovering after rescuing people, including children, inside a house that exploded after they arrived on scene for a domestic incident call in New York City.

topStories.title: Texas executes convicted killer claiming innocence as cousin’s last-minute confession falls flat
topStories.description: James Broadnax was executed in Texas after the Supreme Court denied a last-minute bid to spare his life in a 2008 double killing he said he did not commit.

topStories.title: We might have seen the final straw in the Connor McDavid-Edmonton Oilers marriage
topStories.description: After being bounced in the first round of the Stanley Cup Playoffs Thursday night, the Edmonton Oilers will have some tough questions to face regarding superstar Connor McDavid.

topStories.title: Artemis commander Reid Wiseman describes "dramatic and important warning" while on mission
topStories.description: Commander Reid Wiseman revealed the most difficult decision the crew had to make while in space on the Artemis II mission. Wiseman described a "dramatic and important warning" that came on in the middle of the night.

topStories.title: Goldman Traders Brace for ‘Froth’ Removal as Stocks Get Crowded
topStories.description: After a record-smashing advance in April, US equity bulls are facing a bit of a reckoning: There aren’t many investors left to buy.

topStories.description: Khera had accused Himanta Biswa Sarma’s wife, Riniki Bhuyan Sharma, of possessing multiple foreign passports and undisclosed assets in the US.

topStories.description: The Artemis II crew joined "CBS Mornings" Friday for a live town hall weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.

topStories.title: Trump Vows to Maintain Pressure on Iran Through Naval Blockade | The Pulse 5/1
topStories.description:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Jari Stehn, Goldman Sachs, Chief European Economist; Phillips O’Brien, University of St Andrews, Strategic Studies Professor; Timothy Richards, Vue Founder and CEO.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

topStories.title: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say
topStories.description: Amtrak may ease rules on guns on its trains, sources say. Critics worry that would weaken security even though, authorities say, the accused correspondents' dinner shooter took Amtrak cross-country with his firearms.

dailyNews.title: Miranda Lambert delivers straight-shooting advice to rising country star in surprise bathroom encounter
dailyNews.description: Miranda Lambert told rising country singer Lauren Alaina not to let Nashville change who she is during their first meeting, offering a reality check about the music industry.

dailyNews.title: Fed dissenters explain 'no' votes, saying they disagreed with hinting next move would be a cut
dailyNews.description: Federal Reserve officials who voted this week against the post-meeting statement said they didn't think it was appropriate to signal that the next interest rate move would be lower.

dailyNews.title: WNBA champion Lexie Brown opens up on 'culture shift' since Caitlin Clark's arrival
dailyNews.description: Seattle Storm's Lexie Brown says the WNBA has skyrocketed since Caitlin Clark's 2024 arrival, crediting new fans and financial growth in the league.

dailyNews.title: Pentagon tech chief says Anthropic is still blacklisted, but Mythos is a separate issue
Paula Deen reveals the Kentucky Derby tradition she loves most — and it's not the food or drinks
dailyNews.description: Celebrity chef Paula Deen says the Kentucky Derby's fashion beats its famous food, sharing her love of watching hats and outfits at the entrance gate on Derby Day.

dailyNews.title: Cole Allen clues pile up as Thomas Crooks’ secrets died with him — experts cite evidence gaps in Trump attacks
dailyNews.description: Alleged Trump assassination attempt suspect Cole Allen left a bigger evidence trail than Thomas Crooks, experts say, explaining the information gap.

dailyNews.title: Elon Musk billionaires bill supporters draw progressive challengers in Delaware
dailyNews.description: Musk, whose record pay package was in legal limbo in Delaware, relocated Tesla's incorporation out of state during the spat.

dailyNews.title: Record-breaking DHS shutdown ends. And, May Day protests to draw crowds nationwide
dailyNews.description: Congress has ended the record-breaking shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security. And, May Day demonstrations across the U.S. are expected to draw crowds protesting the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: ‘With folded hands…’: Congress's appeal to Assam CM Himanta over Pawan Khera row, then a 'just begun' counter
dailyNews.description: Abhishek Manu Singhvi said statements attributed to Sarma in the public domain, quoted at length in judgment, were “unrepeatable, unprintable and unstatable”.

dailyNews.title: Trump Vows to Maintain Pressure on Iran Through Naval Blockade
dailyNews.description: Trump reiterated that the blockade of Iranian ports is depriving the Islamic Republic of much-needed oil revenue, claiming the pressure would force Tehran back to the negotiating table.

dailyNews.title: DOJ Releases New Video of Assassination Suspect 'Casing' Hotel
dailyNews.description: The Justice Department released chilling new surveillance video of the man charged with trying to assassinate President Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner. Prosecutors say it shows Cole Allen casing the Washington Hilton the day before the attack. New evidence photos also show bags of ammunition inside his room as well as a gas mask and protective glasses. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

dailyNews.title: Iran warns of "painful retaliation" for any resumption of U.S. strikes
dailyNews.description: As Trump considers his next move, the Iran war — and the Strait of Hormuz — remain gripped in a costly standoff.

topStories.title: Falklands claim: Can Argentina’s Milei use Trump ties to challenge the UK?
topStories.description: As US-UK relations nosedive, questions are mounting over whether Trump might shift Washington's position on the islands.

topStories.title: Poll: Trump’s Iran war reaches Iraq- and Vietnam-era disapproval levels
topStories.description: A Post-ABC-Ipsos poll shows most Americans call the military action a mistake, even as Republicans remain strongly supportive. Many fear the conflict will lead to a recession.

topStories.title: They tracked California hate groups. Now, Trump's DOJ charges donors were defrauded
topStories.description: For years, the Southern Poverty Law Center has been one of the few groups monitoring activity by far-right extremists in California. But the organization was recently charged with fraud and money laundering by the Trump administration.

topStories.title: Trump scraps Scotch whisky tariffs ‘in honor’ of King Charles
topStories.description: The move is expected to recoup millions of dollars a month for Scotland’s economy.

topStories.title: Fill in the blank for the quiz: The Trump admin took aim at _____ this week
topStories.description: This week, the federal government's been busy. There are paint jobs, fresh indictments, commemorative items and more. If you've been paying attention — good job!

topStories.title: Janet Mills stood up to Trump. Democratic voters shrugged.
topStories.description: Liberals say they want a fighter. But a popular governor who clashed with the president failed to catch fire in Maine’s Senate race.

dailyNews.description: Liberals say they want a fighter. But a popular governor who clashed with the president failed to catch fire in Maine’s Senate race.

dailyNews.title: Could your '8647' merch get you in trouble?
dailyNews.description: Florida congressional candidate Mark Davis sells “8647” T-shirts and hats on his campaign website — the same numerical shorthand to express discontent over President Donald Trump, central to the case against a former FBI director.

dailyNews.title: A key Senate rule is slowly losing its grip, and both parties know it
dailyNews.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune has resisted President Donald Trump’s pleas to kill the filibuster, but Republicans circumvented it to try to fund immigration enforcement agencies.

dailyNews.title: It's Day 1 of Medicaid work requirements in Nebraska. People are worried
dailyNews.description: Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.

The foundation’s efforts were overseen by a board of 22 prominent scientists. On Friday last week, they were all fired.…

dailyNews.title: America lost what's 'left of its innocence' after Trump, 'Parks and Rec' star says
dailyNews.description: Former "Parks and Recreation" actor Adam Scott said America lost what was left of its innocence in 2015 when President Donald Trump first entered politics.

dailyNews.title: Top school district put on notice as watchdog group threatens legal action over gender policy
dailyNews.description: A Trump-aligned lawfare group is demanding Fairfax City Public Schools in Virginia rescind policy concealing students' gender identity changes from parents at school, or they will sue.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s 'Economic Fury' squeezes Iran — but can Tehran outlast the pressure?
dailyNews.description: Trump's escalating sanctions, naval blockade and financial enforcement against Iran test whether economic strain can force Iran's regime to bend.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
dailyNews.description: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.

dailyNews.title: Comey Indictment Shows Justice Dept. Got the Message From Bondi’s Firing
dailyNews.description: In naming only an interim successor as acting attorney general, President Trump has established even greater incentives to execute his most extreme demands, current and former officials say.

dailyNews.title: Jared Kushner, Steve Witkoff and the Profitable Business of Peace
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is turning diplomacy into an asset to be leveraged.

dailyNews.title: The World’s Central Banks Are Wrestling With a Gigantic Problem
dailyNews.description: Inflationary surges and economic slowdowns set off by the war in Iran, tariffs and other Trump policies have forced monetary officials to adjust, our columnist says.

dailyNews.title: Fed Officials Cite Inflation Concerns in Defending Dissents
dailyNews.description: The Federal Reserve’s meeting this week was the most divisive in decades, a sign that President Trump’s pick to lead the central bank will face opposition if he pursues substantially lower interest rates.
